zotero.abstractNote: We study the convergence of gradient descent (GD) for logistic regression with data points constrained to the unit sphere. While it is well-known that GD with a constant step-size can diverge for logistic regression on general datasets, we show that when data is restricted to the sphere, GD with a constant step-size converges to the global minimizer for any step-size smaller than 2. This result is surprising as it suggests that the geometry of the data can significantly improve the stability of optimization algorithms. We provide a theoretical analysis of this phenomenon and support our findings with numerical experiments.
